title: How Much of Your Life You Spend Waiting

beats.narration: One thing quietly eats years of your life — waiting. About 3 years of it, just gone.
beats.understand: open on a person stuck in a slow line as the clock spins — 3 years waiting

beats.narration: Six months of it vanish at red lights that never turn green fast enough.
beats.understand: a person stuck in a car at a red light as traffic piles up — 6 months at red lights

beats.narration: Another forty-three days stuck on hold, listening to the same looping music.
beats.understand: a person slumping on the couch, phone to the ear, hold music looping — 43 days on hold

beats.narration: Line after line, light after light, it quietly adds up to years you never get back.
beats.understand: the single data moment: the biggest waiting time-sinks side by side

beats.narration: So stop waiting for your life to start. Get up, and go live it.
beats.understand: the person walks out into a sunrise, arms rising — stop waiting
